   882	    for (auto weakUse : weakUseInsts)
   883	    {
   884	        weakUse->removeAndDeallocate();
   885	    }
   886	}
   887	
   888	Result linkAndOptimizeIR(
   889	    CodeGenContext* codeGenContext,
   890	    LinkingAndOptimizationOptions const& options,
   891	    LinkedIR& outLinkedIR)
   892	{
   893	    SLANG_PROFILE;
   894	
   895	    // This lambda is here so that we can select the correct overload for our parameters, without it
   896	    // the overload deduction fails for passes which have overloads not taking an IRModule*
   897	#define SLANG_PASS(passFunc, ...)                                                          \
   898	    wrapPass(                                                                              \
   899	        codeGenContext,                                                                    \
   900	        #passFunc,                                                                         \
   901	        [](auto* m, auto&&... a) { return passFunc(m, std::forward<decltype(a)>(a)...); }, \
   902	        irModule,                                                                          \
   903	        ##__VA_ARGS__)
   904	
   905	    auto session = codeGenContext->getSession();
   906	    auto sink = codeGenContext->getSink();
   907	    auto target = codeGenContext->getTargetFormat();
   908	    auto targetRequest = codeGenContext->getTargetReq();
   909	    auto targetProgram = codeGenContext->getTargetProgram();
   910	    auto targetCompilerOptions = targetRequest->getOptionSet();
   911	
   912	    // Get the artifact desc for the target
   913	    const auto artifactDesc = ArtifactDescUtil::makeDescForCompileTarget(asExternal(target));
   914	
   915	    // We start out by performing "linking" at the level of the IR.
   916	    // This step will create a fresh IR module to be used for

     1	// slang-pass-wrapper.h
     2	
     3	#ifndef SLANG_PASS_WRAPPER_H
     4	#define SLANG_PASS_WRAPPER_H
     5	
     6	#include "../core/slang-performance-profiler.h"
     7	#include "slang-code-gen.h"
     8	#include "slang-compiler-options.h"
     9	
    10	#include <optional>
    11	#include <type_traits>
    12	
    13	namespace Slang
    14	{
    15	
    16	struct IRModule;
    17	struct CodeGenContext;
    18	
    19	// Standalone hook functions
    20	void prePassHooks(CodeGenContext* codeGenContext, IRModule* irModule, const char* passName);
    21	void postPassHooks(CodeGenContext* codeGenContext, IRModule* irModule, const char* passName);
    22	
    23	// RAII helper for pass hooks and performance profiling
    24	struct PassHooksRAII
    25	{
    26	    CodeGenContext* codeGenContext;
    27	    IRModule* irModule;
    28	    const char* passName;
    29	    std::optional<PerformanceProfilerFuncRAIIContext> perfContext;
    30	
    31	    PassHooksRAII(CodeGenContext* ctx, IRModule* module, const char* name)
    32	        : codeGenContext(ctx), irModule(module), passName(name)
    33	    {
    34	        prePassHooks(codeGenContext, irModule, passName);
    35	
    36	        auto targetRequest = codeGenContext->getTargetReq();
    37	        auto targetCompilerOptions = targetRequest->getOptionSet();
    38	        if (targetCompilerOptions.getBoolOption(CompilerOptionName::ReportDetailedPerfBenchmark))
    39	        {
    40	            perfContext.emplace(passName);
    41	        }
    42	    }
    43	
    44	    ~PassHooksRAII()
    45	    {
    46	        perfContext.reset(); // End profiler timing before post hooks
    47	        postPassHooks(codeGenContext, irModule, passName);
    48	    }
    49	};
    50	
    51	template<typename PassFunc, typename... Args>
    52	auto wrapPass(
    53	    CodeGenContext* codeGenContext,
    54	    const char* passName,
    55	    PassFunc&& passFunc,
    56	    IRModule* irModule,
    57	    Args&&... args)
    58	{
    59	    PassHooksRAII passHooks(codeGenContext, irModule, passName);
    60	    return passFunc(irModule, std::forward<Args>(args)...);
    61	}
    62	
    63	} // namespace Slang
    64	
    65	#endif // SLANG_PASS_WRAPPER_H
    66

   150	
   151	struct GenericDiagnostic;
   152	
   153	class DiagnosticSink
   154	{
   155	public:
   156	    /// Flags to control some aspects of Diagnostic sink behavior
   157	    typedef uint32_t Flags;
   158	    struct Flag
   159	    {
   160	        enum Enum : Flags
   161	        {
   162	            VerbosePath = 0x1, ///< Will display a more verbose path (if available) - such as a
   163	                               ///< canonical or absolute path
   164	            SourceLocationLine =
   165	                0x2,         ///< If set will display the location line if source is available
   166	            HumaneLoc = 0x4, ///< If set will display humane locs (filename/line number) information
   167	            TreatWarningsAsErrors = 0x8, ///< If set will turn all Warning type messages (after
   168	                                         ///< overrides) into Error type messages
   169	            LanguageServer =
   170	                0x10, ///< If set will format message in a way that is suitable for language server
   171	            AlwaysGenerateRichDiagnostics =
   172	                0x20, ///< Convert old style diagnostics to the new style
   173	            MachineReadableDiagnostics =
   174	                0x40, ///< If set will format diagnostics in machine-readable TSV format
   175	        };
   176	    };
   177	
   178	    /// Used by diagnostic sink to be able to underline tokens. If not defined on the
   179	    /// DiagnosticSink, will only display a caret at the SourceLoc
   180	    typedef UnownedStringSlice (*SourceLocationLexer)(const UnownedStringSlice& text);
   181	
   182	    /// Get the total amount of errors that have taken place on this DiagnosticSink
   183	    SLANG_FORCE_INLINE int getErrorCount() { return m_errorCount; }
   184	
   185	    template<typename P, typename... Args>
   186	    bool diagnose(P const& pos, DiagnosticInfo const& info, Args const&... args)
   187	    {
   188	        // If we want to force generation of rich diagnostics do that here
   189	        if (isFlagSet(Flag::AlwaysGenerateRichDiagnostics))
   190	        {
   191	            DiagnosticArg as[] = {DiagnosticArg(args)...};
   192	            return diagnoseRichImpl(getDiagnosticPos(pos), info, (int)sizeof...(args), as);
   193	        }
   194	        else
   195	        {
   196	            DiagnosticArg as[] = {DiagnosticArg(args)...};
   197	            return diagnoseImpl(getDiagnosticPos(pos), info, (int)sizeof...(args), as);
   198	        }
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    template<typename P>
   202	    bool diagnose(P const& pos, DiagnosticInfo const& info)
   203	    {
   204	        // If we want to force generation of rich diagnostics do that here
   205	        if (isFlagSet(Flag::AlwaysGenerateRichDiagnostics))
   206	        {
   207	            return diagnoseRichImpl(getDiagnosticPos(pos), info, 0, nullptr);
   208	        }
   209	        else
   210	        {
   211	            return diagnoseImpl(getDiagnosticPos(pos), info, 0, nullptr);
   212	        }
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    //
   216	    // This template function should be called with the structs generated from
   217	    // slang-diagnostics.lua (defined in slang-rich-diagnostics.h)
   218	    //
   219	    template<typename D>
   220	    bool diagnose(D const& d)
   221	    {
   222	        return diagnoseRichImpl(d.toGenericDiagnostic(), D::getInfo());
   223	    }
   224	
   225	    // Useful for notes on existing diagnostics, where it would be redundant to display the same
   226	    // line again. (Ideally we would print the error/warning and notes in one call...)
   227	    template<typename P, typename... Args>
   228	    bool diagnoseWithoutSourceView(P const& pos, DiagnosticInfo const& info, Args const&... args)
   229	    {
   230	        const auto fs = this->getFlags();
   231	        this->resetFlag(Flag::SourceLocationLine);
   232	
   233	        auto result = diagnose(pos, info, args...);
   234	
   235	        this->setFlags(fs);
   236	        return result;
   237	    }
   238	
   239	    // Add a diagnostic with raw text
   240	    // (used when we get errors from a downstream compiler)
   241	    void diagnoseRaw(Severity severity, char const* message);
   242	    void diagnoseRaw(Severity severity, const UnownedStringSlice& message);
   243	
   244	    /// During propagation of an exception for an internal
   245	    /// error, note that this source location was involved
   246	    void noteInternalErrorLoc(SourceLoc const& loc);
   247	
   248	    /// Create a blob containing diagnostics if there were any errors.
   249	    /// *note* only works if writer is not set, the blob is created from outputBuffer
   250	    SlangResult getBlobIfNeeded(ISlangBlob** outBlob);

    44	    do                                                                                             \
    45	    {                                                                                              \
    46	        (sink)->diagnoseRaw(                                                                       \
    47	            Slang::Severity::Note,                                                                 \

    51	    } while (0)
    52	#define SLANG_UNIMPLEMENTED(sink, pos, what)                                                       \
    53	    do                                                                                             \
    54	    {                                                                                              \
    55	        (sink)->diagnoseRaw(                                                                       \
    56	            Slang::Severity::Note,                                                                 \
    57	            "note: unimplemented triggered at " __FILE__ ":" SLANG_DIAG_STRINGIFY(__LINE__) "\n"); \
    58	        (sink)->diagnose(Slang::Diagnostics::Unimplemented{(what), Slang::getDiagnosticPos(pos)}); \
    59	    } while (0)
    60	#else

    63	#define SLANG_UNIMPLEMENTED(sink, pos, what) \

  1702	
  1703	        case CodeGenTarget::GLSL:
  1704	        case CodeGenTarget::SPIRV:
  1705	        case CodeGenTarget::SPIRVAssembly:
  1706	            // For GLSL targets, we want to translate the vector load/store
  1707	            // operations into scalar ops. This is in part as a simplification,
  1708	            // but it also ensures that our generated code respects the lax
  1709	            // alignment rules for D3D byte-address buffers (the base address
  1710	            // of a buffer need not be more than 4-byte aligned, and loads
  1711	            // of vectors need only be aligned based on their element type).
  1712	            //
  1713	            // Slang IR supports a variant of `Load<T>` on byte-address buffers
  1714	            // that will have greater alignment than required by D3D. The
  1715	            // alignment information is inferred from the operation like a
  1716	            // `Load4Aligned<T>` that returns a `vector<4,T>` that assumes a
  1717	            // `4*sizeof(T)` alignment. We may choose to disable that in favor
  1718	            // of byte-address indexing by setting this flag to true.
  1719	            byteAddressBufferOptions.scalarizeVectorLoadStore = false;
  1720	
  1721	            // For GLSL targets, there really isn't a low-level concept
  1722	            // of a byte-address buffer at all, and the standard "shader storage
  1723	            // buffer" (SSBO) feature is a lot closer to an HLSL structured
  1724	            // buffer for our purposes.
  1725	            //
  1726	            // In particular, each SSBO can only have a single element type,
  1727	            // so that even with bitcasts we can't have a single buffer declaration
  1728	            // (e.g., one with `uint` elements) service all load/store operations
  1729	            // (e.g., a `half` value can't be stored atomically if there are
  1730	            // `uint` elements, unless we use explicit atomics).
  1731	            //
  1732	            // In order to simplify things, we will translate byte-address buffer
  1733	            // ops to equivalent structured-buffer ops for GLSL targets, where
  1734	            // each unique type being loaded/stored yields a different global
  1735	            // parameter declaration of the buffer.
  1736	            //
  1737	            byteAddressBufferOptions.translateToStructuredBufferOps = true;
  1738	            break;
  1739	        case CodeGenTarget::Metal:
  1740	        case CodeGenTarget::MetalLib:
  1741	        case CodeGenTarget::MetalLibAssembly:
  1742	            byteAddressBufferOptions.scalarizeVectorLoadStore = true;
  1743	            byteAddressBufferOptions.treatGetEquivalentStructuredBufferAsGetThis = true;
  1744	            byteAddressBufferOptions.translateToStructuredBufferOps = false;
  1745	            byteAddressBufferOptions.lowerBasicTypeOps = true;
  1746	            break;
  1747	        case CodeGenTarget::WGSL:
  1748	        case CodeGenTarget::WGSLSPIRV:
  1749	        case CodeGenTarget::WGSLSPIRVAssembly:
  1750	            byteAddressBufferOptions.scalarizeVectorLoadStore = true;
  1751	            byteAddressBufferOptions.treatGetEquivalentStructuredBufferAsGetThis = true;
  1752	            byteAddressBufferOptions.translateToStructuredBufferOps = false;
  1753	            byteAddressBufferOptions.lowerBasicTypeOps = true;
  1754	            byteAddressBufferOptions.useBitCastFromUInt = true;
  1755	            break;
  1756	        }
  1757	
  1758	        // We also need to decide whether to translate

     1	#include "slang-ir-check-unsupported-inst.h"
     2	
     3	#include "slang-ir-util.h"
     4	#include "slang-ir.h"
     5	#include "slang-rich-diagnostics.h"
     6	
     7	namespace Slang
     8	{
     9	
    10	void checkUnsupportedInst(TargetRequest* target, IRFunc* func, DiagnosticSink* sink)
    11	{
    12	    SLANG_UNUSED(target);
    13	    for (auto block : func->getBlocks())
    14	    {
    15	        for (auto inst : block->getChildren())
    16	        {
    17	            switch (inst->getOp())
    18	            {
    19	            case kIROp_GetArrayLength:
    20	                sink->diagnose(
    21	                    Diagnostics::AttemptToQuerySizeOfUnsizedArray{.location = inst->sourceLoc});
    22	                break;
    23	            }
    24	        }
    25	    }
    26	}
    27	
    28	void checkUnsupportedInst(IRModule* module, TargetRequest* target, DiagnosticSink* sink)
    29	{
    30	    for (auto globalInst : module->getGlobalInsts())
    31	    {
    32	        switch (globalInst->getOp())
    33	        {
    34	        case kIROp_VectorType:
    35	        case kIROp_MatrixType:
    36	            {
    37	                if (!as<IRBasicType>(globalInst->getOperand(0)) &&
    38	                    !as<IRPackedFloatType>(globalInst->getOperand(0)))
    39	                {
    40	                    sink->diagnose(Diagnostics::UnsupportedBuiltinType{
    41	                        .type = globalInst,
    42	                        .location = findFirstUseLoc(globalInst)});
    43	                }
    44	                break;
    45	            }
    46	        case kIROp_Func:
    47	            checkUnsupportedInst(target, as<IRFunc>(globalInst), sink);
    48	            break;
    49	        case kIROp_Generic:
    50	            {
    51	                auto generic = as<IRGeneric>(globalInst);
    52	                auto innerFunc = as<IRFunc>(findGenericReturnVal(generic));
    53	                if (innerFunc)
    54	                    checkUnsupportedInst(target, innerFunc, sink);
    55	                break;
    56	            }
    57	        default:
    58	            break;
    59	        }
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	} // namespace Slang
    64

    55	// A structure to be used in static data describing different
    56	// diagnostic messages.
    57	struct DiagnosticInfo
    58	{
    59	    int id;
    60	    Severity severity;
    61	    char const* name; ///< Unique name
    62	    char const* messageFormat;
    63	};
    64	
    65	class Diagnostic
    66	{
    67	public:
    68	    String Message;
    69	    SourceLoc loc;
    70	    int ErrorID;
    71	    Severity severity;
    72	
    73	    Diagnostic() { ErrorID = -1; }
    74	    Diagnostic(const String& msg, int id, const SourceLoc& pos, Severity severity)
    75	        : severity(severity)
    76	    {
    77	        Message = msg;
    78	        ErrorID = id;
    79	        loc = pos;
    80	    }

🎬 ACTION
cat > /testbed/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -u
cd /testbed
cat > /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang <<'SLANG'
[anyValueSize(8)]
interface IFoo
{
    float getValue();
}

struct ImplA : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v; }
}

RWByteAddressBuffer byteBuffer;
RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;

[numthreads(1, 1, 1)]
void computeMain()
{
    ImplA a;
    a.v = 7.0;
    IFoo fooA = a;
    byteBuffer.Store<IFoo>(0, fooA);

    IFoo loaded = byteBuffer.Load<IFoo>(0);
    outputBuffer[0] = loaded.getValue();
}
SLANG

./build/Release/bin/slangc /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang -target spirv -stage compute -entry computeMain -conformance "ImplA:IFoo=1" -o /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.spv
spirv_status=$?
echo spirv_status=$spirv_status
./build/Release/bin/slangc /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang -target hlsl -stage compute -entry computeMain -conformance "ImplA:IFoo=1" -o /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.hlsl
hlsl_status=$?
echo hlsl_status=$hlsl_status
if [ "$spirv_status" -ne 0 ] || [ "$hlsl_status" -ne 0 ]; then
    exit 1
fi
EOF
chmod +x /testbed/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh
/testbed/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh

    // Interface/existential values have a runtime representation that is
    // produced by existential lowering. The generic ByteAddressBuffer path
    // currently runs after that lowering, so accepting interface element types
    // here leaves dynamic-dispatch IR inside the lowered load/store expansion
    // and backends can ICE while emitting it. Diagnose each offending IR
    // instruction once and then replace loads with a dummy value / drop stores
    // so later passes do not see the unsupported operation.
    HashSet<IRInst*> m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores;
' --insert_line 47

    50	    // produced by existential lowering. The generic ByteAddressBuffer path
    51	    // currently runs after that lowering, so accepting interface element types
    52	    // here leaves dynamic-dispatch IR inside the lowered load/store expansion

    bool containsInterfaceType(IRType* type)
    {
        if (!type)
            return false;
        if (as<IRInterfaceType>(type))
            return true;

        switch (type->getOp())
        {
        case kIROp_ArrayType:
        case kIROp_UnsizedArrayType:
            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRArrayTypeBase>(type)->getElementType());
        case kIROp_VectorType:
            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRVectorType>(type)->getElementType());
        case kIROp_MatrixType:
            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRMatrixType>(type)->getElementType());
        default:
            break;
        }

        if (auto structType = as<IRStructType>(type))
        {
            for (auto field : structType->getFields())
            {
                if (containsInterfaceType(field->getFieldType()))
                    return true;
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    void diagnoseUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStore(IRInst* inst, IRType* type)
    {
        if (m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores.contains(inst))
            return;

        m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores.add(inst);
        m_sink->diagnose(
            inst->sourceLoc,
            kUnsupportedInterfaceTypeForByteAddressBufferLoadStore,
            (IRInst*)type);
    }
' --insert_line 140

diff --git a/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh b/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh
new file mode 100755
index 000000000..2262489da
--- /dev/null
+++ b/repro_ifoo_byteaddr.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -u
+cd /testbed
+cat > /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang <<'SLANG'
+[anyValueSize(8)]
+interface IFoo
+{
+    float getValue();
+}
+
+struct ImplA : IFoo
+{
+    float v;
+    float getValue() { return v; }
+}
+
+RWByteAddressBuffer byteBuffer;
+RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;
+
+[numthreads(1, 1, 1)]
+void computeMain()
+{
+    ImplA a;
+    a.v = 7.0;
+    IFoo fooA = a;
+    byteBuffer.Store<IFoo>(0, fooA);
+
+    IFoo loaded = byteBuffer.Load<IFoo>(0);
+    outputBuffer[0] = loaded.getValue();
+}
+SLANG
+
+./build/Release/bin/slangc /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang -target spirv -stage compute -entry computeMain -conformance "ImplA:IFoo=1" -o /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.spv
+spirv_status=$?
+echo spirv_status=$spirv_status
+./build/Release/bin/slangc /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.slang -target hlsl -stage compute -entry computeMain -conformance "ImplA:IFoo=1" -o /tmp/ifoo_byteaddr.hlsl
+hlsl_status=$?
+echo hlsl_status=$hlsl_status
+if [ "$spirv_status" -ne 0 ] || [ "$hlsl_status" -ne 0 ]; then
+ (exit 1)
+fi
diff --git a/source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp b/source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp
index 83a249030..54aa9402b 100644

@@ -16,6 +16,13 @@
 namespace Slang
 {
 
+
+static const DiagnosticInfo kUnsupportedInterfaceTypeForByteAddressBufferLoadStore = {
+    41301,
+    Severity::Error,
+    "unsupportedInterfaceTypeForByteAddressBufferLoadStore",
+    "interface/existential type '$0' is not supported in ByteAddressBuffer Load/Store; use a StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer or serialize a concrete type instead"};
+
 bool isCPUTarget(TargetRequest* targetReq);
 
 // As is typical for IR passes in Slang, we will encapsulate the state
@@ -39,6 +46,16 @@ struct ByteAddressBufferLegalizationContext
     IRModule* m_module;
     IRBuilder m_builder;
 
+    // Interface/existential values have a runtime representation that is
+    // produced by existential lowering. The generic ByteAddressBuffer path
+    // currently runs after that lowering, so accepting interface element types
+    // here leaves dynamic-dispatch IR inside the lowered load/store expansion
+    // and backends can ICE while emitting it. Diagnose each offending IR
+    // instruction once and then replace loads with a dummy value / drop stores
+    // so later passes do not see the unsupported operation.
+    HashSet<IRInst*> m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores;
+
+
     // Track ByteAddressBuffer parameters that have been replaced by StructuredBuffer.
     // These need to be removed at the end to avoid leaving invalid types in the IR
     // for targets like SPIR-V that don't support ByteAddressBuffer directly.
@@ -121,6 +138,51 @@ struct ByteAddressBufferLegalizationContext
     // The logic for both the load and store cases is similar,
     // so we will present the entire load case first and then
     // move on to stores.
+
+    bool containsInterfaceType(IRType* type)
+    {
+        if (!type)
+            return false;
+        if (as<IRInterfaceType>(type))
+            return true;
+
+        switch (type->getOp())
+        {
+        case kIROp_ArrayType:
+        case kIROp_UnsizedArrayType:
+            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRArrayTypeBase>(type)->getElementType());
+        case kIROp_VectorType:
+            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRVectorType>(type)->getElementType());
+        case kIROp_MatrixType:
+            return containsInterfaceType(cast<IRMatrixType>(type)->getElementType());
+        default:
+            break;
+        }
+
+        if (auto structType = as<IRStructType>(type))
+        {
+            for (auto field : structType->getFields())
+            {
+                if (containsInterfaceType(field->getFieldType()))
+                    return true;
+            }
+        }
+
+        return false;
+    }
+
+    void diagnoseUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStore(IRInst* inst, IRType* type)
+    {
+        if (m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores.contains(inst))
+            return;
+
+        m_diagnosedUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStores.add(inst);
+        m_sink->diagnose(
+            inst->sourceLoc,
+            kUnsupportedInterfaceTypeForByteAddressBufferLoadStore,
+            (IRInst*)type);
+    }
+
     //
     void processLoad(IRInst* load)
     {
@@ -135,6 +197,15 @@ struct ByteAddressBufferLegalizationContext
         if (isTypeLegalForByteAddressLoadStore(type))
             return;
 
+        if (containsInterfaceType(type))
+        {
+            m_builder.setInsertBefore(load);
+            diagnoseUnsupportedInterfaceLoadStore(load, type);
+            load->replaceUsesWith(m_builder.emitDefaultConstruct(type));
+            load->removeAndDeallocate();
+            return;
+        }
+
         // If the type is one that requires legalization,
         // then we will set up to insert new IR instructions
         // to replace it.
